mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP, as measured by GDPC1 on a year-over-year basis, indicates expanding aggregate demand which supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the rolling 4-quarter Rank IC between GDPC1 YoY growth and 60-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.0 or the hit rate drops below 50% over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate dropping to or below 4.5% signals a tight labor market that historically precedes wage inflation and monetary tightening, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If at least 20 instances of UNRATE <= 4.5% occur with positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: A low federal funds rate combined with rising consumer prices creates a negative real interest rate environment that incentivizes risk-taking and capital flows into equities, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If periods where the real interest rate (FEDFUNDS minus CPIAUCSL YoY inflation) is negative for at least one month show a negative median 20-day SPX return over the full sample.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle defined by the federal funds rate rising above 0.5% and exceeding its 6-month moving average, further rate increases compress equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If in at least 10 tightening cycle episodes the average 60-day forward SPX return is not negative or the hit rate of negative returns is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly retail and food services sales (RSAFS) provide a timely measure of consumer demand; positive year-over-year growth signals robust consumption, supporting corporate earnings and leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the correlation between RSAFS YoY growth and forward 20-day SPX returns is not significantly positive (p-value >0.05) over a 10-year rolling history.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month increase in industrial production exceeding 0.5% for two consecutive months reflects strengthening manufacturing activity, which boosts economic output expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month change > 0.5% for two consecutive months
failure_condition: If triggered instances (INDPRO mom >0.5% for 2 months) have a median 20-day SPX return that is not positive over any 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Joint strength in nonfarm payroll growth and housing starts, both on a year-over-year basis, indicates a broad-based economic expansion that is supportive of corporate profits, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a 60 trading day horizon.
failure_condition: If periods when both PAYEMS YoY and HOUST YoY are positive (or both above their respective medians) produce a negative median 60-day SPX return in more than 30% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: In a low inflation environment where CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation is below 2%, the absence of overheating allows the Federal Reserve to maintain accommodative policy, supporting equity valuations and leading to positive short-term S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If triggered low inflation instances (CPIAUCSL YoY < 2%) yield negative average 5-day SPX returns over any 3-year window.
